description: Transform NanoClaw from a single-agent assistant into a multi-agent team with PM agents (per-project, Slack-based), a Marketer agent, and inter-agent communication. Agents communicate via Slack @mentions, PM agents manage GitHub repos via host-side Codex CLI with read-only project code mounts, and the Marketer handles SNS content with approval flows.

userStories.title: Multi-channel agent registration schema
userStories.description: Modify the `registered_groups` table to allow one agent (folder) to be registered in multiple Slack channels. Remove the UNIQUE constraint on `folder`. Add migration that preserves existing data. Update `getAllRegisteredGroups()` to return a structure that supports multiple JIDs per folder. Update `registerGroup` IPC handler and MCP tool to support this. Add a `role` field to the `register_group` MCP tool parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-001: `registered_groups` table no longer has UNIQUE constraint on `folder` column. Migration preserves existing rows.
REQ-002: A helper function `getAgentsByChannel(jid: string): RegisteredGroup[]` returns all agents registered to a given channel JID.
REQ-003: A helper function `getChannelsForAgent(folder: string): string[]` returns all JIDs an agent is registered to.
REQ-004: `register_group` MCP tool accepts optional `role` parameter and passes it through IPC to the host registration handler.
REQ-005: Existing tests pass (run `npm test`). New unit tests cover multi-folder registration scenarios in `src/db.ts`.

userStories.title: Multi-agent message routing per channel
userStories.description: Update the message loop in `src/index.ts` to handle channels with multiple registered agents. When a message arrives in a shared channel (e.g., #dobby-marketer), check ALL registered agents for that JID. For agents with `requiresTrigger=true`, match the trigger pattern to decide which agent(s) should process the message. If multiple agents match (unlikely but possible), process in priority order. Update `processGroupMessages` to accept a `(chatJid, agentFolder)` tuple rather than just `chatJid`. Update `GroupQueue` to track per-folder state (not per-JID) to prevent concurrent containers for the same agent folder.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-006: `startMessageLoop` iterates over all (jid, agent) pairs, not just JIDs. A message in a shared channel is offered to each registered agent for trigger matching.
REQ-007: `GroupQueue` uses `folder` (not `chatJid`) as the concurrency key. Two JIDs for the same folder share a single queue slot.
REQ-008: `processGroupMessages(chatJid, agentFolder)` fetches messages for the specific chatJid but routes to the specific agent folder's container.
REQ-009: When an agent is in multiple channels, messages from ALL its channels accumulate and are processed together (or sequentially per-channel). No messages are lost.

userStories.title: Cross-agent messaging via IPC + Slack
userStories.description: Add a new MCP tool `send_agent_message` that lets an agent send a message to another agent. The message is: (1) posted to the target agent's Slack channel for human visibility, (2) stored directly in the DB with `is_cross_agent=1` so it bypasses bot message filters. Add `is_cross_agent` column to `messages` table. Modify `getNewMessages` and `getMessagesSince` to include cross-agent messages (where `is_cross_agent=1`). Add `agent_source` column to track who sent it. Add `maxPingPongTurns` limit (default 5) to prevent infinite loops between agents — tracked via a counter stored in message metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-010: New `is_cross_agent INTEGER DEFAULT 0` and `agent_source TEXT` columns on `messages` table with migration.
REQ-011: `getNewMessages` and `getMessagesSince` return rows where `is_bot_message=0 OR is_cross_agent=1`.

REQ-012: New MCP tool `send_agent_message(target_agent: string, text: string, channel_jid?: string)` writes IPC task. Host resolves target agent's channel, posts to Slack, stores in DB with `is_cross_agent=1` and `agent_source=<sender_folder>`.

REQ-013: A `ping_pong_count` is tracked per (source_agent, target_agent, thread) tuple. When count exceeds `MAX_PING_PONG_TURNS` (5), further cross-agent messages in that thread are blocked with a warning logged.
REQ-014: Cross-agent messages include the sender agent name in the content prefix (e.g., `[from @marketer]`) so the receiving agent knows who sent it.

userStories.title: Channel member injection into agent prompts
userStories.description: Before invoking an agent container, call Slack API `conversations.members` for the agent's channel(s). Resolve each member via `users.info` to get display name and bot status. Format as a preamble block injected into the prompt. Cache member lists for 5 minutes to avoid excessive API calls. Include both humans and other registered agents with their roles.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-015: New function `getChannelMembers(channelId: string, botToken: string): Promise<ChannelMember[]>` returns `{ userId, displayName, isBot, agentRole? }` for all members.
REQ-016: Member list is cached per channel with 5-minute TTL. Cache is stored in-memory (Map).
REQ-017: The prompt preamble includes a `[Channel members]` block listing each member with their role (human/agent) and mentionable identifier.
REQ-018: For agents in multiple channels, the preamble lists members for the channel the current message came from.

userStories.title: Host-side Codex MCP endpoint and mount exclusions
userStories.description: Add new IPC task types for host-side Codex and git operations. The host manages a directory of cloned repositories (configurable path, default `~/.nanoclaw/repos/`). New IPC task types: `codex_exec` (run codex in a repo), `git_create_branch`, `git_checkout`, `git_pull`, `gh_create_pr`, `gh_issue_list`, `gh_issue_comment`. Each agent's `containerConfig.envVars.ALLOWED_REPOS` specifies which repos it can access (comma-separated). Corresponding MCP tools are added to `ipc-mcp-stdio.ts` so agents can call them from inside containers. Additionally, extend `AdditionalMount` interface with `excludePatterns` field and implement exclusion via tmpfs overlays in `container-runner.ts` so PM agents can mount project repos read-only without including heavy dependency directories.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-019: New IPC task type `codex_exec` with fields: `repo`, `prompt`, `branch?`. Host validates repo is in agent's `ALLOWED_REPOS`, then runs `codex exec --full-auto --sandbox danger-full-access --cd <repo_path> "<prompt>"`. Result (stdout) written to IPC response file that agent reads.
REQ-020: New IPC task types `git_create_branch(repo, branch)`, `git_checkout(repo, branch)` that run git commands on the host repo clone.
REQ-021: New IPC task types `gh_create_pr(repo, title, body, base, head)`, `gh_issue_list(repo, state?)`, `gh_issue_comment(repo, issue_number, body)` using `gh` CLI on host.
REQ-022: Authorization: each request validates `repo` against agent's `ALLOWED_REPOS` env var. Unauthorized requests are logged and rejected.

REQ-023: Corresponding MCP tools added to `ipc-mcp-stdio.ts`: `codex_exec`, `git_create_branch`, `git_checkout`, `git_pull`, `gh_create_pr`, `gh_issue_list`, `gh_issue_comment`. Each writes IPC task file and polls for response.

REQ-024: IPC response mechanism: host writes result to `/workspace/ipc/responses/<request_id>.json`. MCP tool polls for this file (timeout 10 minutes for codex_exec, 30 seconds for git/gh operations).
REQ-055: New IPC task type `git_pull(repo, branch?)` runs `git pull origin <branch>` (default: current branch) on the host repo clone. Keeps mounted code in sync with upstream.
REQ-056: `AdditionalMount` interface extended with optional `excludePatterns: string[]` field (e.g., `["node_modules", ".venv", "dist", ".git/objects"]`).

REQ-057: `buildVolumeMounts()` in `container-runner.ts` generates `--tmpfs` flags for each exclude pattern path under the mount's container path, effectively masking those directories with empty tmpfs. Example: mount at `/workspace/extra/autorag-research:ro` + `--tmpfs /workspace/extra/autorag-research/node_modules`.

userStories.title: PM agent registration and CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: Create the PM agent infrastructure for autorag-research. Register the agent to Slack channel `C09RELR4R9N` with role `pm-agent` and display name "Young-gu". Each PM agent has a customizable display name set via the `name` field in registration and the `trigger` pattern (e.g., `@young-gu`). The `getAssistantLabel()` in `slack.ts` uses the trigger to format outbound messages with the agent's name. Create `groups/pm-autorag/CLAUDE.md` with PM agent instructions (project context, GitHub repo, responsibilities). Create `groups/pm-autorag/schedule.json` with GitHub issue polling task. Set `containerConfig.envVars` with `GITHUB_REPO` and `ALLOWED_REPOS=autorag-research`.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-025: Agent registered in DB: `jid=slack:C09RELR4R9N, folder=pm-autorag, name=Young-gu, trigger=@young-gu, role=pm-agent, requiresTrigger=false`.
REQ-026: `groups/pm-autorag/CLAUDE.md` exists with PM agent persona, responsibilities (GitHub issue triage, implementation via Codex, PR reviews, SecondBrain insights), and project-specific context.
REQ-027: `groups/pm-autorag/schedule.json` contains a GitHub issue polling task running every 15 minutes (cron: `*/15 * * * *`) that calls `gh_issue_list` and processes new issues.
REQ-028: `containerConfig.envVars` includes `GITHUB_REPO` and `ALLOWED_REPOS` values.

REQ-058: `containerConfig.additionalMounts` includes the project repo mounted read-only at `/workspace/extra/autorag-research/` with `excludePatterns: ["node_modules", ".venv", "dist", ".git/objects", "__pycache__", ".mypy_cache", ".pytest_cache"]`.

REQ-059: `groups/pm-autorag/CLAUDE.md` includes instructions: "Your project code is mounted read-only at /workspace/extra/autorag-research/. Browse code to understand context before crafting Codex prompts. Use git_pull to sync latest changes from upstream."

userStories.title: PM GitHub issue polling and triage
userStories.description: Create a skill or prompt template for PM agents to poll GitHub issues and respond. The PM uses the `gh_issue_list` MCP tool to check for new/updated issues. For each new issue, PM: (1) posts a triage comment via `gh_issue_comment`, (2) assesses if the issue is detailed enough for implementation, (3) writes a SecondBrain insight. If the issue is actionable, PM creates a plan and delegates to Codex (US-008). The polling schedule is defined in `schedule.json`.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-029: PM agent can call `gh_issue_list` and receives a parsed list of issues (number, title, body, labels, state).
REQ-030: For each new issue (not previously seen), PM posts a triage comment acknowledging the issue and providing initial assessment.
REQ-031: PM tracks seen issues using a local file (`groups/pm-autorag/seen_issues.json`) to avoid duplicate processing.
REQ-032: PM writes a SecondBrain insight for each new issue with type `pm-insight`, including issue details and triage decision.

userStories.title: PM Codex integration for implementation
userStories.description: When a PM agent determines an issue is detailed enough for implementation, it orchestrates the full workflow: (1) `git_pull` to sync latest code, (2) read relevant source files from `/workspace/extra/{repo}/` to understand the codebase context, (3) create a feature branch via `git_create_branch`, (4) craft a detailed Codex prompt referencing specific files, functions, and patterns found in the code, (5) invoke `codex_exec` with that prompt, (6) create a PR via `gh_create_pr` linking the issue. The PM reads code first and constructs Codex prompts with specific implementation goals, file paths, existing patterns, and acceptance criteria extracted from the issue. The PM agent's CLAUDE.md includes guidelines for when to implement vs when to ask for more details.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-033: PM can invoke the full workflow: git_pull → code review → branch creation → codex_exec → PR creation in sequence.

REQ-034: The Codex prompt includes: issue title, issue body, specific file paths and code snippets from the mounted project, implementation goals, acceptance criteria extracted from the issue, and project conventions.
REQ-035: The PR body references the issue with "Closes #N" syntax and includes a summary of changes.
REQ-036: If codex_exec fails or times out, PM posts a comment on the issue explaining the failure and tags it for human attention.

REQ-060: Before crafting the Codex prompt, PM reads relevant source files from the mounted project code (`/workspace/extra/{repo}/`) to understand existing patterns, dependencies, and architecture. The prompt references these specific patterns so Codex produces consistent code.

userStories.title: PM PR review via subagent
userStories.description: PM agents can invoke a Reviewer subagent to review PRs. The reviewer is defined in `.nanoclaw/subagents.json` inside the PM group folder. When a PR is created (by Codex or externally), PM can ask the Reviewer to analyze the diff and post review comments directly on the GitHub PR via `gh` CLI. The review follows a structured format: summary, issues found, suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-037: `groups/pm-autorag/.nanoclaw/subagents.json` defines a `reviewer` subagent with a code review prompt.
REQ-038: Reviewer can read PR diff via `gh pr diff <number>` (executed through IPC/MCP).
REQ-039: Reviewer posts review comments on the PR via `gh pr review <number> --comment --body "<review>"`.
REQ-040: Review format includes: summary, list of issues (severity: critical/warning/info), and an overall assessment.

userStories.title: Marketer agent registration and infrastructure
userStories.description: Create the Marketer agent. Register it to a dedicated `#marketer` Slack channel. Create `groups/marketer/CLAUDE.md` with marketer persona (SNS marketing, self-branding, content creation using hypeboy skills). Create `groups/marketer/schedule.json` with periodic research tasks. Also register Marketer to a shared channel with Dobby for cross-agent communication.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-041: Marketer agent registered in DB for its dedicated channel with `role=marketer, requiresTrigger=false`.
REQ-042: `groups/marketer/CLAUDE.md` exists with marketer persona, available hypeboy skills (X, LinkedIn, Threads, Reddit), content guidelines, and self-branding strategy instructions.
REQ-043: `groups/marketer/schedule.json` contains periodic research tasks (e.g., daily SNS trend check, weekly content planning).
REQ-044: Marketer is also registered to a shared channel with Dobby for cross-agent communication, with `trigger=@marketer`.

userStories.title: Marketer content approval flow
userStories.description: Implement the approval workflow in the #marketer channel. When the Marketer creates content, it posts a draft to the channel with a clear "DRAFT" label and content preview. The user can approve by reacting with a checkmark emoji or typing "승인"/"approve". The Marketer watches for approval reactions/messages, and upon approval, publishes to the target SNS platform. Implement this as: (1) Marketer posts draft via `send_message`, (2) approval detection in `slack.ts` for emoji reactions, (3) approval triggers Marketer to publish.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-046: `slack.ts` handles `reaction_added` events. When a checkmark reaction is added to a Marketer draft message in the #marketer channel, store an approval message in DB that triggers the Marketer agent.
REQ-047: Marketer can also detect "승인" or "approve" text replies to a draft thread as approval.
REQ-048: Upon approval, Marketer uses the appropriate hypeboy skill to publish content. The published URL is posted back to the #marketer channel.
REQ-049: Marketer can autonomously reply to comments/mentions on published SNS posts without requiring approval.

userStories.title: WhatsApp to Slack reference cleanup
userStories.description: Audit and update all code, documentation, prompts, and CLAUDE.md files that reference WhatsApp as the primary channel. Replace with Slack references. This includes the main CLAUDE.md, README.md mentions, any container CLAUDE.md templates, and inline comments in source code. Do NOT remove WhatsApp channel code — it should remain as a fallback option — but all default references and documentation should indicate Slack as primary.
acceptanceCriteria: REQ-050: `CLAUDE.md` (project root) describes Slack as the primary channel, not WhatsApp.
REQ-051: `README.md` updated if it references WhatsApp as the default setup.
REQ-052: Any group CLAUDE.md templates reference Slack channel communication.
REQ-053: `src/index.ts` comment/log messages that reference WhatsApp are updated to be channel-agnostic or mention Slack.
REQ-054: WhatsApp channel code (`src/channels/whatsapp.ts`) remains functional but is clearly marked as optional/legacy.
